findings.ruleDescription: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

matches.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where user-controlled input is used as a URL in server-side requests. In MCP servers, this could allow agents to probe internal networks, access cloud metadata endpoints, or reach otherwise-restricted services.
findings.recommendation: This code makes HTTP requests using externally-controlled URLs without apparent validation. This could allow probing internal networks or accessing cloud metadata endpoints. Implement URL allowlisting and block private ranges.

fullDescription.text: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.

Detects patterns where user-controlled input is used as a URL in server-side requests. In MCP servers, this could allow agents to probe internal networks, access cloud metadata endpoints, or reach otherwise-restricted services.

matchDetails.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

properties.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

This code makes HTTP requests using externally-controlled URLs without apparent validation. This could allow probing internal networks or accessing cloud metadata endpoints. Implement URL allowlisting and block private ranges.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could enable path traversal attacks, allowing access to files outside intended directories. Common in MCP servers that handle file operations without proper path sanitization.
findings.recommendation: This code accesses file system paths using external input without apparent sanitization. An attacker could use path traversal (../) to read or write files outside the intended directory. Review file access patterns carefully.

matches.description: path.join with external input without validation

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns that could enable path traversal attacks, allowing access to files outside intended directories. Common in MCP servers that handle file operations without proper path sanitization.

matchDetails.description: path.join with external input without validation

properties.recommendation: This code accesses file system paths using external input without apparent sanitization. An attacker could use path traversal (../) to read or write files outside the intended directory. Review file access patterns carefully.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects files with unusually high Shannon entropy, indicating obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. Legitimate code typically has entropy between 3.5-5.0. Values above 5.5 for non-binary text files suggest hidden content, base64 blobs, or packed malware.
findings.reportTitle: High entropy content detected — possible obfuscation
findings.recommendation: This file has unusually high information density (entropy), which often indicates obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. While minified JavaScript is common, obfuscated code in agent skills is suspicious and should be reviewed manually.

matches.description: File content Shannon entropy exceeds 6.0 — likely obfuscated

fullDescription.text: Detects files with unusually high Shannon entropy, indicating obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. Legitimate code typically has entropy between 3.5-5.0. Values above 5.5 for non-binary text files suggest hidden content, base64 blobs, or packed malware.

message.text: High entropy content detected — possible obfuscation

matchDetails.description: File content Shannon entropy exceeds 6.0 — likely obfuscated

properties.recommendation: This file has unusually high information density (entropy), which often indicates obfuscated, packed, or encrypted content. While minified JavaScript is common, obfuscated code in agent skills is suspicious and should be reviewed manually.

findings.ruleTitle: Dynamic code execution via eval or Function constructor
findings.ruleDescription: Detects usage of eval(), new Function(), or similar dynamic code execution patterns that allow arbitrary code to run. In MCP servers and tool code, these are rarely legitimate and often indicate payload delivery mechanisms.
findings.recommendation: Review the context of eval/Function usage carefully. In MCP servers, legitimate uses are extremely rare. This likely indicates a code injection vector.

matches.description: new Function() to create executable code from string

rules.name: Dynamic code execution via eval or Function constructor
